strategy_description: Local IV smile: bisection IV on each VEV, quadratic fit in log-moneyness on closest 6 strikes to EMA(S); BS fair per strike with extrapolated wing IV; MM edge 2 on vouchers, extract, hydrogel. DTE from round3description + plot_iv_smile_round3 (CSV day d: DTE_open=8-d, intraday wind-down); csv_day inferred from opening extract mid (5250/5245/5267.5). Smile refit every 80 ticks for runtime.
Same as iteration 0 but CLOSEST_K=3 (parameter sweep); tape analysis suggested k=6 had lower IV RMSE than k=3 but backtest PnL was worse, so k=6 retained for primary line.

Same closest-k=6 local smile thesis, but voucher quote size scales with local BS vega and quote edge scales inversely with vega regime. This keeps wings quoted (small size, wider edge) while concentrating size near ATM where local-surface pricing is most stable; extract/hydrogel MM unchanged.

Vega-scaled local-smile MM (parent: v2). Parameter sweep: BASE_EDGE_VEV=2 and REFIT_EVERY=50 (vs 3 and 80) so default quotes are one tick tighter relative to the wide median spreads on deep OTM/ITM strikes (see spread_median_by_product.json) and the fitted surface updates more often as extract moves. Extract/hydrogel and closest-k=6 fit unchanged.

Parent v3: add two-tier log-moneyness edge bonus (|log K/S|>0.10: +2 ticks; >0.15: +1 more) on top of vega-based edge, to match very wide top-of-book spreads on deep wings (see spread_median_by_product.json).

Parent v3: lighter single-step wing rule — +2 edge ticks only if |log(K/S)|>0.12 (avoids widening the 0.10–0.15 moneyness band that still shows meaningful volume). Simpler than v4; still not beating v3 on total PnL.
Parent v3: grid on closest-k for local surface fit only — CLOSEST_K=5 (between k=3 and k=6). Same vega/edge, REFIT_EVERY, extract/hydrogel as v3. Backtest below v3; k=6 remains best in this line.

Parent v3 random-older-style sweep: CLOSEST_K=4 with all other v3 mechanics unchanged (vega-sized quotes, BASE_EDGE_VEV=2, REFIT_EVERY=50). This tests whether a tighter local subset helps in fast regimes; performance dropped materially, so k=6 remains preferred.

Parent v3 (best PnL): only REFIT_EVERY=40 (vs 50) for slightly more frequent local smile refits. Same k=6, vega sizing, edges, extract/hydrogel. Theta tape check (see analysis) showed time-decay risk concentrated near 5200-5300; this sweep tests whether more frequent IV re-fit helps — it did not beat v3.

Family 13 pivot: 3-regime state machine from RV-IV z-score (calm/neutral/stressed) computed on extract returns vs near-ATM IV. Keep local smile valuation and vega sizing from v3, but multiply voucher quote width by regime (calm 0.8x, neutral 1.0x, stressed 1.35x). Gating is intentionally simple/interpretable.

Family-13 nearby but distinct variant from v9 to reduce duplicate risk: add regime hysteresis + persistence gate. Regime enters calm/stressed only past +/-1.0 z and exits to neutral inside +/-0.35; width and voucher size multipliers vary by regime (calm tighter/smaller, stressed wider/larger). Keeps local-smile valuation interpretable.

Family-13 refinement from v10/v9, still RV-IV regime with simple gating: keep hysteresis + persistence, and add strike elasticity/shock-spread-informed width adjustment (from response_spread_asymmetry analysis). Edge widened by moneyness where beta_up/down asymmetry and post-shock spread widening are higher; size still regime-scaled.

Parent v9: same RV-IV 3-regime + local smile. Add extract shock overlay: when |d extract mid| >= 2 (Round3 tape p90), add 1 tick to voucher edge and scale size x0.88 — motivated by higher mean |dIV| at ATM after shocks and corr(|dS|,|dIV|) ~0.26 (atm_iv_dS_lead_lag.json).

Parent v12. Parameter sweep from voucher_lead_lag_shock.json: on |dU|>=2 shock, add strike-tiered extra edge (+2/5000, +1/5100-5300, 0/5400+5000) and size mult (0.82 / 0.88 / 0.92 / 0.95) by absolute mean |dV| on shock rows — heavier where voucher jump size is largest. Tests whether scaling the flat v12 shock helps.

Parent v12. Second grid point: same +1 tick and x0.88 as v12, but only on strikes 5000–5300 where pooled same-tick corr(dU,dV) is strongest (~0.62–0.75); other strikes skip shock overlay. Wing strikes still use regime+vega only.
